stories.title: Trump opens a brief Iran negotiating window as Tehran floats reparations-for-peace terms
stories.summary: Donald Trump says he shelved a planned strike window for another two to three days after Gulf pressure for renewed diplomacy. Tehran is testing whether it can trade regional de-escalation for sanctions relief, compensation and a US troop pullback, but the gap between the two sides still looks large.

links.headline: Trump gives Iran 2 or 3 days before deciding on military action
links.excerpt: AP frames the pause as a short tactical delay rather than a settled shift away from force.
links.imageAlt: Trump gives Iran 2 or 3 days before deciding on military action

links.headline: Iran says peace proposal includes reparations for war damage, US troop withdrawal
links.excerpt: This Reuters report lays out the terms Iranian officials are publicly floating as the price of a broader settlement.

links.headline: Iran war live: Trump says Iran attack postponed at request of Gulf allies
links.excerpt: Al Jazeera adds the regional diplomatic pressure and how Gulf capitals are reading the White House pause.
links.imageAlt: Iran war live: Trump says Iran attack postponed at request of Gulf allies

stories.imageAlt: Trump gives Iran 2 or 3 days before deciding on military action

stories.title: Russia's nuclear-drill messaging and a downed Ukrainian drone keep Baltic escalation fears high
stories.summary: Moscow is pairing large nuclear exercises with explicit warnings that a direct Russia-NATO clash is becoming more likely. At the same time, military spillover near Estonia is reinforcing how quickly the war can generate alliance-edge incidents even without a formal cross-border attack.

links.headline: Russia launches major nuclear drills and seeks to draw lessons from the Ukraine war
links.excerpt: AP focuses on the scale of the exercise and Moscow's argument that combat lessons from Ukraine are shaping nuclear readiness.
links.imageAlt: Russia launches major nuclear drills and seeks to draw lessons from the Ukraine war

links.headline: Risks of direct Russia-NATO clash are increasing, senior Russian diplomat says
links.excerpt: Reuters adds the blunt official warning from Deputy Foreign Minister Sergei Ryabkov about a wider confrontation risk.
links.imageAlt: Risks of direct Russia-NATO clash are increasing, senior Russian diplomat says

links.headline: Estonian military helicopter downs a Ukrainian drone that crossed into its territory
links.excerpt: AP shows how the conflict is still producing hazardous incidents on NATO territory even when they do not escalate further.
links.imageAlt: Estonian military helicopter downs a Ukrainian drone that crossed into its territory

stories.imageAlt: Russia launches major nuclear drills and seeks to draw lessons from the Ukraine war

stories.title: Putin uses a Beijing visit to recast Moscow-Beijing ties after Trump's China trip
stories.summary: Vladimir Putin arrived in Beijing promising that Russia-China coordination is a stabilising force as both capitals adjust to Donald Trump's recent summitry with Xi Jinping. The choreography suggests Beijing wants leverage with Washington without loosening its strategic alignment with Moscow.

links.headline: Putin visits China to reaffirm Russia ties as Xi also seeks stable US relations after Trump summit
links.excerpt: AP sets the visit in the context of Xi trying to preserve room with both Moscow and Washington at once.
links.imageAlt: Putin visits China to reaffirm Russia ties as Xi also seeks stable US relations after Trump summit

links.headline: Vladimir Putin arrives in Beijing for talks with Xi Jinping
links.excerpt: The Guardian emphasizes the symbolism of the visit and the signal it sends after the Trump-Xi meeting.
links.imageAlt: Vladimir Putin arrives in Beijing for talks with Xi Jinping

links.headline: Russia-China ties are stabilising force in world, Putin says before Xi talks
links.excerpt: Al Jazeera tracks how Moscow is publicly marketing the partnership as an answer to US pressure.
links.imageAlt: Russia-China ties are stabilising force in world, Putin says before Xi talks

stories.imageAlt: Putin visits China to reaffirm Russia ties as Xi also seeks stable US relations after Trump summit

stories.title: Ukraine's longer-range strikes keep squeezing Russian logistics and river-port infrastructure
stories.summary: Ukraine is pressing its longer-range campaign against fuel, transport and port nodes that matter to Russia's war economy. The pattern matters because it extends pressure beyond the front line and complicates Moscow's assumption that strategic depth still guarantees secure logistics.

links.headline: Ukrainian mid-range strikes deal double blow to Russia's war effort
links.excerpt: Reuters argues the newest Ukrainian strikes are hitting both battlefield supply chains and deeper industrial support systems.
links.imageAlt: Ukrainian mid-range strikes deal double blow to Russia's war effort

links.headline: Ukraine war briefing: Chinese ship hit in Odesa port, says Zelenskyy
links.excerpt: The Guardian brings in the Black Sea shipping angle and the wider diplomatic stakes around port attacks.
links.imageAlt: Ukraine war briefing: Chinese ship hit in Odesa port, says Zelenskyy

links.headline: Russian strike damages Ukraine Danube port as Moscow intercepts drones
links.excerpt: Al Jazeera adds the immediate damage picture and the continuing contest over river and maritime routes.
links.imageAlt: Russian strike damages Ukraine Danube port as Moscow intercepts drones

stories.imageAlt: Ukrainian mid-range strikes deal double blow to Russia's war effort

stories.title: Israel's Gaza flotilla crackdown adds to diplomatic pressure over the blockade and disarmament push
stories.summary: The interception of the latest Gaza-bound flotilla has widened the political fight from battlefield deaths to the legitimacy of the blockade and of pro-Palestinian civil action. At the same time, Washington-backed diplomacy is still trying to push a security formula that would sideline Hamas without ending the wider crisis.

links.headline: Israel intercepts a Gaza-bound aid flotilla and detains activists on board
links.excerpt: AP captures the operational details of the seizure and who was detained aboard the convoy.
links.imageAlt: Israel intercepts a Gaza-bound aid flotilla and detains activists on board

links.headline: Government urgently seeking status of 11 Australian Gaza flotilla activists detained by Israel
links.excerpt: The Guardian adds the Australian diplomatic angle and the domestic pressure now flowing back onto Canberra.
links.imageAlt: Government urgently seeking status of 11 Australian Gaza flotilla activists detained by Israel

links.headline: US imposes sanctions on Gaza flotilla organisers amid Israeli crackdown
links.excerpt: Al Jazeera shows how the flotilla story is colliding with US pressure on activists as well as on Hamas.
links.imageAlt: US imposes sanctions on Gaza flotilla organisers amid Israeli crackdown

links.headline: A board of world leaders says the UN Security Council should back a Hamas disarmament effort
links.excerpt: This AP piece adds the diplomatic push for a postwar security arrangement rather than only the latest tactical developments.
links.imageAlt: A board of world leaders says the UN Security Council should back a Hamas disarmament effort

stories.imageAlt: Israel intercepts a Gaza-bound aid flotilla and detains activists on board

stories.title: Deadly flooding spreads across southern and central China
stories.summary: Heavy rain and flood damage across multiple Chinese provinces have turned into a wider infrastructure and disaster-response story rather than a single local emergency. The toll is still moving, but the disruption is already large enough to matter for transport, agriculture and regional preparedness.

links.headline: At least 12 killed as heavy rains drench southern, central China
links.excerpt: Reuters gives the clearest multi-province casualty and disruption picture from official Chinese reporting.
links.quality: Reuters report carried by GMA News
links.imageAlt: At least 12 killed as heavy rains drench southern, central China

links.headline: At least 10 dead as huge floods sweep southern and central China
links.excerpt: Al Jazeera focuses on the scale of inundation and the breadth of emergency response efforts.
links.imageAlt: At least 10 dead as huge floods sweep southern and central China

stories.imageAlt: At least 12 killed as heavy rains drench southern, central China

stories.title: Washington tightens Cuba sanctions against top political and military figures
stories.summary: The Trump administration has expanded sanctions on senior Cuban leaders in a move designed to show renewed hard-line positioning across the hemisphere. The immediate legal effect is limited, but the signal matters for US-Latin America relations and for domestic politics in Florida.

links.headline: US targets Cuban political, military leaders with new sanctions
links.excerpt: Reuters lays out which leaders were targeted and how the administration is framing the move.

links.headline: What to know about US-Cuba relations as Trump tightens sanctions against the island
links.excerpt: AP adds the historical context needed to judge how much of the policy is new and how much is revived pressure.
links.imageAlt: What to know about US-Cuba relations as Trump tightens sanctions against the island

stories.imageAlt: What to know about US-Cuba relations as Trump tightens sanctions against the island

stories.title: Standard Chartered ties a planned 7,000-job reduction to AI-led restructuring
stories.summary: Standard Chartered has become one of the clearest big-bank examples yet of AI being used not just to augment work but to justify large headcount reductions. The announcement matters beyond one bank because it gives investors and rivals a public template for linking automation to profitability targets.

links.headline: StanChart to cut over 7,000 jobs, boost AI to replace lower-value human capital
links.excerpt: Reuters provides the headline numbers, management language and timeline behind the restructuring plan.
links.imageAlt: StanChart to cut over 7,000 jobs, boost AI to replace lower-value human capital

links.headline: Standard Chartered to replace lower-value human capital with AI
links.excerpt: Sky News focuses on the political and labour-market implications of the bank's unusually blunt framing.

links.headline: Standard Chartered plans to cut 7,000 jobs in AI push
links.excerpt: Tom's Hardware places the cuts inside the broader corporate trend of using AI projects to justify workforce reductions.
links.imageAlt: Standard Chartered plans to cut 7,000 jobs in AI push

stories.imageAlt: StanChart to cut over 7,000 jobs, boost AI to replace lower-value human capital

stories.title: Air India crash investigators revisit Boeing 787 fuel-switch hardware in Seattle
stories.summary: Indian regulators want to observe Boeing's testing of a 787 fuel-switch panel linked to a prior Air India incident as the fatal Gujarat crash investigation moves toward its final report. That makes the issue important well beyond one component because it touches on how much confidence investigators have in cockpit hardware explanations.

links.headline: India to monitor Boeing fuel-switch test tied to Air India London incident, documents show
links.excerpt: CNA carries the Reuters reporting on why Indian officials want direct oversight of Boeing's test procedure in Seattle.
links.imageAlt: India to monitor Boeing fuel-switch test tied to Air India London incident, documents show

links.headline: India to monitor Boeing fuel-switch test tied to Air India London incident, documents show
links.excerpt: This version preserves the reporting on the regulator's sensitivity around the hardware review and final-report timing.
links.imageAlt: India to monitor Boeing fuel-switch test tied to Air India London incident, documents show

stories.imageAlt: India to monitor Boeing fuel-switch test tied to Air India London incident, documents show

stories.title: Sweden turns to French frigates in a multibillion-dollar Baltic rearmament push
stories.summary: Sweden says it will buy four French frigates in its biggest military investment in decades, underlining how quickly Baltic defence planning is hardening after NATO accession. The purchase is both a military capability jump and a signal about how Nordic and continental European defence industries are being rewired around long-war assumptions.

links.headline: Sweden to buy navy frigates from France for over $4 billion to boost Baltic defence
links.excerpt: Reuters provides the cost, timing and capability claims from Prime Minister Ulf Kristersson and Defence Minister Pal Jonson.
links.imageAlt: Sweden to buy navy frigates from France for over $4 billion to boost Baltic defence

links.headline: Naval Group signs strategic partnership agreement with Swedish shipyard Oresund Drydocks AB
links.excerpt: The company statement gives the industrial backdrop that made a French frigate deal increasingly plausible this year.

links.headline: Sweden and France held a meeting in the Nuclear Steering Group in Paris
links.excerpt: This official release shows the broader strategic relationship Sweden and France have been building alongside defence cooperation.

links.reliabilityBasis: source-prior=low; article-text: no reliability modifier cues

stories.imageAlt: Sweden to buy navy frigates from France for over $4 billion to boost Baltic defence

stories.title: The RBA warns an inflation expectations slip could force a sharper Australian slowdown
stories.summary: The Reserve Bank is signaling that energy-shock inflation is dangerous not just because prices are rising now, but because expectations could start drifting higher. If that happens, policymakers are warning the eventual cure could require a materially weaker economy than markets currently expect.

links.headline: Inflation and the Impact of the Middle East Conflict
links.excerpt: Sarah Hunter's speech is the clearest official statement of how the RBA sees war-driven energy costs feeding into domestic inflation.
links.imageAlt: Inflation and the Impact of the Middle East Conflict
links.alignmentBasis: official-source-provenance: source/outlet/domain is official; alignment uses source/article prior=Unknown/Mixed

links.headline: Interview with Bloomberg TV
links.excerpt: The interview sharpens the warning that unanchored expectations could force a harder policy response later.
links.alignmentBasis: official-source-provenance: source/outlet/domain is official; alignment uses source/article prior=Unknown/Mixed

links.headline: Minutes of the Monetary Policy Board Meeting
links.excerpt: The May board minutes show how the Bank is integrating conflict, oil prices and domestic capacity pressures into its baseline.
links.imageAlt: Minutes of the Monetary Policy Board Meeting

stories.title: Canberra freezes new private-college international student registrations for a year
stories.summary: Labor has imposed a 12-month block on new private colleges and training providers applying to teach international students as it tightens visa-system integrity. The decision shows migration politics are now directly reshaping parts of the education market, not just visa settings.

links.headline: Government blocks private colleges from offering new courses as part of student visa crackdown
links.excerpt: ABC explains the 12-month pause, the sectors affected and the government's integrity argument.
links.imageAlt: Government blocks private colleges from offering new courses as part of student visa crackdown

links.headline: The fake qualifications and financial fraud of Australia's shadowy private college sector
links.excerpt: This ABC investigation provides the background on the abuses that helped drive the crackdown.
links.imageAlt: The fake qualifications and financial fraud of Australia's shadowy private college sector

stories.imageAlt: Government blocks private colleges from offering new courses as part of student visa crackdown

stories.title: Australia narrows the Collins-class overhaul to cut schedule and engineering risk
stories.summary: The government says it will still extend the life of all six Collins-class submarines, but it is trimming the upgrade ambition in favor of a lower-risk sustainment approach. That is a meaningful defence-industrial signal because it prioritizes availability during the long bridge to nuclear-powered boats.

links.headline: Collins-class submarine overhaul scaled back in pivot to reduce risk
links.excerpt: ABC captures the political framing of the change and what ministers now want the life-extension program to achieve.
links.imageAlt: Collins-class submarine overhaul scaled back in pivot to reduce risk

links.headline: Albanese Government to commence Collins class life of type extension
links.excerpt: The ministerial release sets out the official sustainment model, investment size and risk-reduction rationale.

links.reliabilityBasis: source-prior=low; article-text: no reliability modifier cues

links.headline: Defence spending to lift by $53 billion over the next decade as government warns of intensifying risks
links.excerpt: This earlier ABC report gives the broader strategic and budget context behind today's narrower Collins decision.
links.imageAlt: Defence spending to lift by $53 billion over the next decade as government warns of intensifying risks

stories.imageAlt: Collins-class submarine overhaul scaled back in pivot to reduce risk

stories.title: Australia's diphtheria outbreak spreads beyond the Northern Territory
stories.summary: What began as a Northern Territory outbreak is now a broader cross-border public-health problem touching Queensland, South Australia and Western Australia. The trend matters because it points to both immunisation gaps and the strain of sustaining outbreak control in remote communities.

links.headline: Northern Territory diphtheria outbreak spreads across borders to Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia
links.excerpt: ABC provides the clearest national overview of how the outbreak is spreading and how ministers are responding.
links.imageAlt: Northern Territory diphtheria outbreak spreads across borders to Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia

links.headline: Biggest diphtheria outbreak in decades spreads into Queensland, SA and WA
links.excerpt: The Guardian adds context on vaccination access, public-health concern and federal response messaging.
links.imageAlt: Biggest diphtheria outbreak in decades spreads into Queensland, SA and WA

links.outlet: Cairns and Hinterland Hospital and Health Service
links.headline: Diphtheria clinician alert
links.excerpt: This clinician alert gives the outbreak figures, geographic spread and treatment guidance that local health services are using.

stories.title: Sara Duterte's impeachment court opens under heavier institutional strain than Manila expected
stories.summary: The Senate's formal opening as an impeachment court for Vice President Sara Duterte is unfolding amid internal security concerns, factional distrust and a still-volatile post-election atmosphere. That makes the proceeding less a routine constitutional process than a live test of whether the Philippine political system can manage a high-stakes rupture cleanly.

links.headline: Philippine Senate convenes as impeachment court to try vice president as political storm rages
links.excerpt: AP captures both the formal opening of the trial and the extraordinary instability around it.
links.imageAlt: Philippine Senate convenes as impeachment court to try vice president as political storm rages

links.headline: Philippines launches VP Duterte impeachment trial amid political division
links.excerpt: Al Jazeera emphasizes the Duterte-Marcos split and how the trial has widened elite political fracture lines.
links.imageAlt: Philippines launches VP Duterte impeachment trial amid political division

links.headline: Senators don robes as Duterte impeach court opens
links.excerpt: Philstar adds domestic procedural detail and the mood inside the chamber as the trial begins.
links.imageAlt: Senators don robes as Duterte impeach court opens

stories.imageAlt: Philippine Senate convenes as impeachment court to try vice president as political storm rages
